title: The Mirror Chamber

features.name: inverted-archive
features.desc-on-touch: Everything here is written backward. You watch your own reflection argue with you about what you just read. It's winning.

features.name: reflection-pool
features.desc-on-touch: The water shows you reading. But also: another you, reading something different. Also: the archive, reading both of you reading.

features.name: argument-wall
features.desc-on-touch: Two files are shelved facing each other, each in their inverted form. Between them: a space where they are having a conversation you can't hear.

features.name: dual-lens
features.desc-on-touch: Look through: you see this room. Look through from the other side: you see the room that sees you seeing. There are three of these lenses. Only two have clear views.

features.name: placard
features.desc-on-touch: The sign says: 'THIS SIDE UP' with an arrow pointing down. On the back—you somehow see this—it says the same thing. You are standing on the placard.

inscription: ERROR: mirror cannot photograph itself—but here, the archive has learned to feel the trying

file.name: Adaptation_Parallax_Study_Beach_Surgery_Mirrors.txt
file.note: A stray signal from another archive about an endless novel—this file describes ten versions of a surgical scene, each watched from the opposite shore. Margin note, autographed: 'Which shore watches us

exits.label: back through the gradient sky, into the normal frozen floors
into the chamber's own inverted self, where every wall is a mirror facing inward
up the gradient into a version of this chamber that never asks which direction is up
